summary: Summary: Speaker1, who had been standing in the wrong line for an hour, repeatedly asked for help and grew increasingly agitated (frustration). Speaker2 first explained where Speaker1 should go and repeatedly directed her to the correct line while remaining neutral (neutral). As the interaction continued, both speakers displayed mounting tension: Speaker1 struggled to get the form filled and pressed for assistance (frustration), and Speaker2 responded that he could not help and pointed to the long line behind them (frustration). When Speaker1 demanded managerial intervention and accused staff of dishonesty she escalated further (anger); Speaker2, revealed as the manager, stayed firm and at times more forceful, moving from a neutral stance into frustration and brief anger (neutral → frustration → anger), reiterating that rules prevented any special treatment and even suggesting security if necessary. The conversation ended with Speaker2 directing Speaker1 to the proper line and Speaker1 left still upset (frustration/anger).
